mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor demand that compresses consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, depressing equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of instances following UNRATE breaches above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST does not predict negative 60-day forward returns more often than chance (binomial p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising consumer credit balances reflect household leverage that becomes unsustainable when borrowing costs remain elevated, triggering equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth under FEDFUNDS > 4.5% does not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in walk-forward testing (Sharpe ratio of signal < -0.2).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity that supports corporate earnings revisions, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive relationship with PAYEMS growth rates over any 2-year rolling window (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 3.5% signals an overheating economy that forces aggressive monetary tightening, which historically compresses equity valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and flat housing starts indicates sector-specific supply bottlenecks that increase input costs without corresponding demand growth, squeezing margins and equity returns.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-HOUST divergence metric fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment remains below 5.0% while the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, tight labor markets sustain wage inflation that forces prolonged restrictive policy, ultimately triggering equity corrections.
failure_condition: The joint condition of low unemployment and high rates does not produce negative 5-day forward returns more than 55% of the time in out-of-sample testing.
